demonstration: MCP returned the issue body with HTML entities (\u0026#34; = " = ") inside the agent-meta JSON block. Calling json.loads() on the extracted JSON string returned None silently because " is not a valid JSON quote character.
discovered_at_message: Real-world finding #4 — the MCP issue_read returns the body with HTML-escaped quotes (" instead of "). The agent-meta parser will fail on this.

demonstration: Every comment posted via mcp__github__add_issue_comment gets the trailer appended unconditionally. The handler's strict json.loads() fails because '---' and the markdown line are not valid JSON suffixes.
discovered_at_message:
Real-world bug #5 — major. The MCP add_issue_comment tool automatically appends '

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)_' to every comment.

demonstration: ToolSearch for 'comment update edit modify' returned no MCP tool that edits a comment. add_issue_comment creates new ones; there is no patch/edit equivalent.
implication: The protocol's spec §9.4 step 7 ('Edit the comment to set agent_ack: finished') is not implementable by an MCP-only agent. Workaround options: (a) post a follow-up ack comment; (b) update agent-meta in issue body instead; (c) skip ack and document the gap.

demonstration: mcp__github__issue_write supports method=create and method=update only. There is no method=lock or separate lock_issue tool. Locking is handled by the lock-and-sweep workflow (which has REST access via GITHUB_TOKEN).
implication: Agent cannot lock or unlock issues. Workflows can. Don't design protocols where the agent needs to lock state.

demonstration: Tried mcp__github__create_branch with branch='agent/harness-01-5f9cab5d/sub-alpha' from feature='agent/harness-01-5f9cab5d'. Got 422. Git refs cannot have a name and a sub-namespace at the same prefix level — refs use the filesystem and 'agent/harness-01-5f9cab5d' would have to be both a file and a directory.
fix: Use a flat separator. The session settled on '--sub-' (double-dash). New pattern: agent/harness-01-5f9cab5d--sub-alpha

evidence_raw: POST /repos/.../issues/N/comments → 403 "Unable to create comment because issue is locked."
demonstration: The protocol's batch-job-handler workflow has if: github.event.issue.locked == true, requiring locked issues. But locked issues refuse comments from GITHUB_TOKEN even with permissions: issues: write. The handler's required output (writing the terminal envelope back) is impossible.
fix: Drop the lock at issue creation. Let lock-and-sweep apply only the label. Lock at issue close instead, after the workflow has done all its writes. The workflow's other filters (label + author) provide the injection-guard role.
discovery_quote: GitHub's API returns a 403 with body: 'Unable to create comment because issue is locked.' Locked issues only accept comments from repo collaborators. github-actions[bot] is not treated as a collaborator on a locked issue.

demonstration: WebFetch on /actions/runs/<id> hits 'Sign in to view logs' even on public repos. No MCP tool exposes /repos/.../actions/runs/<id>/logs. Subagent-driven debug needed marker comments + base64-embedded stdout to diagnose handler crash.
fix: Ship the diagnostic patterns from live-debug-from-mcp-only skill. They are the only way an MCP-only operator can see what happened in a CI run.

demonstration: Config had a placeholder. Workflow YAML's if: clause used vars.AGENT_LOGIN which was unset. The if: silently evaluated false on every comment. MCP-authenticated user is jonathanmanton, not my-bot.
fix: Source actual login at session start via mcp__github__get_me, persist to config, reference in workflow YAML directly.

demonstration: Issue with >30 comments needs Link: rel="next" pagination. The MCP list_comments wraps it; the underlying RestGitHubClient implemented in this session paginates explicitly via _next_link helper.
fix_code_snippet:
while url:
    resp = session.get(url)
    items.extend(resp.json())
    url = next_link_from_header(resp.headers.get('Link'))

evidence_raw: After updating issue #11 body via mcp__github__issue_write, calling issue_read returned the body with " entities again. Round-trip is not trailer-only — the unescape is needed on EVERY read.
lesson: html.unescape is required at every parse-from-MCP boundary, even for content the agent itself wrote. The MCP server escapes on output, not just on display.

demonstration: github.event.issue is fully present in issue_comment.created webhooks (locked, labels, user, etc.). Workflow if: filters can use these without an extra API call.
lesson: Don't add a separate REST call to fetch issue state in the workflow's if: — it's already in github.event.issue.

demonstration: close-on-merge.yml needed permissions: contents: write to delete branches via REST. Even though branch deletion sounds metadata-ish, GitHub considers it a 'contents' operation.
fix: Always grant contents: write on workflows that delete branches, modify files, or create commits.

evidence_raw: git push origin --delete <branch> → 403 'Branch deletion is not allowed: <branch>'
demonstration: The local git proxy at 127.0.0.1:35501 blocks all ref-deletion requests, regardless of namespace. Only avenue for branch deletion is the REST API via a workflow.
fix: Drive deletions through the system's own auto-cleanup workflow. Push an empty commit, open a PR, squash-merge — close_on_merge sweeps the branch via REST.
